response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile, experienced engineer with a genuine developer-tooling bent — he even wrote C/C++ components for an actual IDE (NI LabWindows/CVI) and has built test runners and assertion libraries. However, the role's core requirements are strong Java and Kotlin expertise, deep JVM understanding, and solid Spring ecosystem experience, and his career is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and PHP. Java appears only marginally (robotics work in 2009-2011), Kotlin and Spring not at all, and his IDE experience is roughly seven months, nearly two decades ago. The gap on the hard requirements is too large for a mid-senior JVM tooling position.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience building C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI
Strong developer-tooling track record: test runner (Trial), assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), MCP code generators
Proven work on complex, high-scale systems (low-latency services handling millions of requests per second)
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin experience and only marginal, dated Java exposure; no evidence of deep JVM knowledge
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or other enterprise Java framework experience
IDE-specific work was brief (~7 months) and in a C ecosystem, ending in 2008
